metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace (or supplement) the merge-time ticket comment with a durable repo artifact — e.g., a `docs/g7-promotion-plan.md` committed in the PR — so the promotion-path documentation is verifiable via `git log` or a `make test` file-existence check.
findings.finding: AC4's 'in-session proof' is a merge-time comment on the ticket, which is not a repo artifact verifiable by CI and is a one-time transition action rather than a durable code-state. No proving command exists for this deliverable, making it manually inspectable only.
findings.location: AC4 — 'In-session proof: a comment on this ticket documents the promotion-decision PATH — the fire-rate/validity query, the 0.6–0.7 promotion band + trigger, and where the decision is recorded.'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to Scope: 'commit `docs/g7-promotion-plan.md` containing (a) the REVIEW_RESULT fire-rate/validity query, (b) the 0.6–0.7 promotion trigger definition, and (c) the pointer to 72b6-b0d2-2f93-456d.' Then update AC4 to: 'In-session proof: `docs/g7-promotion-plan.md` exists in the PR and contains all three promotion-path items (verified by a file-existence + content-anchor test or a CI lint rule).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `applies()` (registry.py lines 311-339) and its callers to the edit-target list, extending the function to accept a `has_parent_id` boolean and the `applies_at` schema to support a `require_parent` condition — so G7 is skipped on parentless leaves, not just childless ones.
findings.finding: The `applies()` function in registry.py (lines 311-339) has no mechanism for a `has_parent_id` condition — it supports `scope` (container vs leaf), `suppress_types`, and `suppress_when` only. The plan gates G7 on '(ticket has a parent_id) AND (ticket has no children)' via `applies_at`, but `scope: ["leaf"]` alone only gates on `not has_children`, not on `has parent_id`. A parentless leaf (a top-level task with no children) would also satisfy `scope: ["leaf"]` and would incorrectly receive G7. The plan names `applies_at` as the mechanism but does not name `applies()` as an edit target requiring extension to carry a `has_parent_id` dimension.
findings.location: What section — 'ROUTING — fires ONLY on leaf tickets: applies_at gates on (ticket has a parent_id) AND (ticket has no children)'; registry.py `applies()` lines 311-339
findings.suggested_fix: Add `has_parent_id: bool = False` to `applies()`'s signature and a corresponding `require_parent` key to the `applies_at` schema. Update `criteria_routing.json` G7 entry with `"require_parent": true`. Name `applies()` explicitly as an edit target in the Scope section.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `route_criteria()` (orchestrator.py lines 337-375) and its consumers `workflow_ops.py` and `production_batch_runner.py` to the Scope edit-target list, updating them to pass `has_parent_id` from `ctx.state` into `applies()`.
findings.finding: `route_criteria()` in orchestrator.py (lines 337-375) calls `registry.applies()` and would need to pass `has_parent_id` presence to honour G7's parent-gating — but it is not named as an edit target. Similarly, `workflow_ops.py` (line 151, which calls `route_criteria`) and `production_batch_runner.py` are unenumerated consumers of the routing path that would need to propagate `has_parent_id` from `ctx.state`.
findings.location: Scope section — names orchestrator.py `_assemble_context_uncached` but not `route_criteria`; orchestrator.py lines 337-375; workflow_ops.py line 151

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify whether parent_plan reaches G7 via pre-injected `extra_context` (the path agent criteria actually traverse: `_chunk` → `_pass1_with_ladder` → `pass1_chunk`) or via G7's own agentic `show_ticket` call — `_ticket_graph_blob` feeds only ISF and is the wrong injection point for an AGENT-tier criterion.
findings.finding: The plan says `parent_plan` is surfaced into G7's context 'via `pass1._ticket_graph_blob` (pass1.py:281)'. However, `_ticket_graph_blob` is called ONLY from `run_pass1` for the ISF criterion (pass1.py line 489), feeding `pass1_isf` — not the agent-criteria path. G7 runs via the `_chunk([crit], True)` path (pass1.py line 449), which goes through `_pass1_with_ladder` → `passes.pass1_chunk` using `extra_context`. If parent_plan must be pre-injected (not fetched by the agent's own `show_ticket` tool), the injection point is `extra_context`, not `_ticket_graph_blob`.
findings.location: Context Assembly section — 'surface it into the G7 criterion's context via `pass1._ticket_graph_blob` (pass1.py:281)'; pass1.py lines 281-302 and 489
findings.suggested_fix: If pre-injection is intended: add parent_plan to `extra_context` in `run_pass1` for G7's agent chunk specifically (mirroring how `decomp_context` is injected only for G5-bearing chunks, pass1.py line 418). If agent self-fetch is intended: document this in the plan and remove `_ticket_graph_blob` as the surfacing point for G7.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State explicitly how the `has parent_id` half of the G7 leaf gate is implemented — either extend `applies()` with a new `require_parent_id: true` routing key or add a new `suppress_when` predicate that reads `ctx.state.get('parent_id')`, and name which in the Scope section.
findings.finding: The `applies()` function in registry.py has no `require_parent_id` axis — it gates on `scope` (container/leaf), `suppress_types`, and `suppress_when`, but cannot express 'only when a parent_id is present'. The plan says G7 applies when '(ticket has a parent_id) AND (ticket has no children)', but the second condition (has parent_id) is not expressible in the current routing schema, so the described leaf gate cannot be implemented without extending `applies()` or adding a new routing key. The plan does not name how this extension is accomplished.
findings.location: What section — routing, applies_at gate; registry.py `applies()` function (lines 311–339)
findings.suggested_fix: Add `require_parent_id: true` to the `applies_at` schema and extend `registry.applies()` to check `ctx.state.get('parent_id') is not None` (passing `ctx` or the state dict as an argument) when that key is present. Alternatively, add a `suppress_when: no_parent_id` condition with a matching predicate in `applies()`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify HOW the `parent_id`-presence gate is expressed in the routing schema — e.g. add a `suppress_when: parentless_leaf` condition to `applies_at` (requiring a new predicate in `applies()` and a new `is_parentless_leaf(plan, ctx)` helper), OR implement it as a code-level guard in `route_criteria()` — and verify this mechanism against the `_validate_routing_entry` validation contract.
findings.finding: The plan asserts `applies_at` can gate G7 on `(ticket has a parent_id) AND (ticket has no children)`, but the existing routing schema and `applies()` function have no mechanism for a `parent_id`-presence predicate — `scope` only supports `[container|leaf]` and `suppress_when` only has `test_task`/`mechanical_leaf` conditions; the `_validate_routing_entry` validator enforces this closed vocabulary and would silently ignore or reject an unrecognized `applies_at` key.
findings.location: What / ROUTING section; Scope (registry.py `applies_at` gate); AC line 1 (routing test)
findings.suggested_fix: Add a `suppress_when: ["parentless_leaf"]` condition to G7's `applies_at` entry, implement `is_parentless_leaf(plan, ctx)` in registry.py (returns True when `ctx.state.get('parent_id')` is falsy), and extend `applies()` to check it. This mirrors the existing `mechanical_leaf`/`test_task` suppress_when pattern and requires no schema validation changes beyond adding the new condition name to the allowed vocabulary.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify WHERE the `has_parent_id` half of the G7 gate is enforced: either (a) add a `require_parent_id: true` key to the `applies_at` schema and a corresponding branch in `applies()`, or (b) declare that G7's rubric PASS-not-applicable on a missing `parent_plan` field, or (c) name it a `suppress_when` condition — pick one and make it the named edit target.
findings.finding: The `applies_at` gate the plan specifies for G7 requires a dual condition — `(ticket has a parent_id) AND (ticket has no children)` — but the existing `applies()` function in registry.py accepts only `has_children`, `ticket_type`, and `plan`; the `applies_at` routing schema has no `require_parent_id` (or equivalent) key. Adding `"scope": ["leaf"]` to the G7 routing entry handles the no-children half but leaves the has-parent_id condition with no specified enforcement point — not in `applies_at` schema, not in `applies()`, and not described as rubric-internal logic.
findings.location: What section — 'ROUTING — fires ONLY on leaf tickets: applies_at gates on (ticket has a parent_id) AND (ticket has no children)' / registry.py applies() line 311
findings.suggested_fix: The lowest-blast option: in the G7 rubric, add a GATE sentence — 'If the injected `parent_plan` context is absent or empty, emit PASS not-applicable immediately' — and document that `parent_plan` is populated only when the ticket has a parent_id (so an absent field IS the gate). This avoids changing the `applies()` signature or the routing schema.

findings.finding: AC4's 'the promotion decision path is documented on this ticket' is a post-implementation editorial action with no in-session proving command or artifact spec — it cannot be verified at claim time and is not a durable code/repo end-state.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria, AC4
findings.suggested_fix: Add to Scope: 'a `docs/decisions/g7-promotion-criteria.md` file documenting the field-data thresholds and window that will trigger the blocking promotion review.' Then the AC becomes: 'The promotion criteria file exists at docs/decisions/g7-promotion-criteria.md and states the observation window and fire-rate/adjudication threshold for promotion.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify HOW the 'has parent_id' routing condition is implemented — either extend `applies()` with a `require_parent_id` applies_at key (and update `route_criteria` to pass `parent_id`) or add a pre-filter in `route_criteria` before calling `applies()` — so the implementation path is unambiguous.
findings.finding: The plan states G7's `applies_at` gates on '(ticket has a parent_id) AND (ticket has no children)', but the existing `applies()` function in registry.py only receives `has_children`, `ticket_type`, and `plan` — it has no way to evaluate `has parent_id`. The `applies_at` schema currently supports `scope`, `suppress_types`, and `suppress_when`; none of these can express 'has a parent_id'. The plan does not specify how this new routing condition is wired into the existing machinery.
findings.location: Scope section: 'registry.py (register G7 in CANONICAL_LLM/AGENT_TIER/CODEBASE_GROUNDED; applies_at leaf gate)'; What section: 'applies_at gates on (ticket has a parent_id) AND (ticket has no children)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a `require_parent_id: true` field to G7's `applies_at` routing entry, extend `applies()` to accept an optional `parent_id: str | None` parameter, and pass `ctx.state.get('parent_id')` from `route_criteria`. This is the minimal, schema-consistent extension.

findings.finding: The `applies_at` schema and `registry.applies()` have no `has_parent_id` axis, so the plan's required routing gate ('ticket has parent_id AND no children') cannot be expressed declaratively today. The plan hedges this as 'if not expressible declaratively → registry.py' but does not commit to a design: (a) add a new `requires_parent` key to the `applies_at` schema and extend `applies()`, (b) add an imperative branch in `route_criteria()` (precedent: ISF special-case), or (c) rely on the AGENT rubric to self-gate via PASS not-applicable for parentless leaves. Options (a) and (b) require extending the routing machinery; option (c) would silently run the criterion on ALL leaves (incurring AGENT-tier cost) and rely on the model to self-suppress — a different posture than the plan's AC ('a routing test proves it is skipped for parentless tickets'). The AC explicitly demands a routing-level skip, which rules out (c), making the required extension un-specified.
findings.location: Scope section: 'src/rebar/llm/plan_review/registry.py (applies_at leaf gate if not expressible declaratively)'; AC1: 'a routing test proves it is skipped for parentless tickets'
findings.suggested_fix: Add `requires_parent: true` to the G7 `applies_at` block in criteria_routing.json, extend `registry.applies()` to accept `has_parent_id: bool = False` and check `if ap.get('requires_parent') and not has_parent_id: return False`, then thread `has_parent_id=bool(ctx.state.get('parent_id'))` through the three call sites (orchestrator.route_criteria, workflow_ops.plan_review_assemble_criteria, production_batch_runner). This is purely additive — existing criteria have no `requires_parent` key so the default (False → no constraint) is backward-compatible.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve the contradiction between the plan's 'truncate each to a char cap' and `PlanContext`'s documented 'never truncated, never content-chunked' invariant — either specify the cap with a grounded value (and update the invariant's documentation) or eliminate the truncation by having G7 fetch parent context at runtime (where the agent's own context window is the natural bound).
findings.finding: The plan introduces content truncation ('truncate each to a char cap; no whole-subtree dumps') into PlanContext, directly contradicting the explicit 'never truncated, never content-chunked, by design' invariant in both `PlanContext`'s docstring (det_floor.py:96-98) and the orchestrator's module docstring (orchestrator.py:8). The char cap value is unspecified, creating new unnamed config surface.
findings.location: What section: 'BOUNDED (truncate each to a char cap; no whole-subtree dumps)' and Scope section: 'det_floor.py PlanContext (add parent_plan field)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve the `PlanContext` 'always whole' docstring contract conflict: either place `parent_plan` in a separate context struct/parameter (not on `PlanContext`) or explicitly update the docstring to document the truncation carve-out for `parent_plan`.
findings.finding: The new `parent_plan` field on `PlanContext` is described as 'bounded (truncate each to a char cap)' — but `PlanContext`'s docstring explicitly states 'content is ALWAYS whole (no truncation, no content-chunking, by design)'. This is a semantic contract violation in the plan that should be resolved: either the `parent_plan` field lives outside `PlanContext` (passed separately to G7's context injection) and the dataclass docstring stays true, or the docstring must be updated to carve out the `parent_plan` field. The plan does not acknowledge this contradiction.
findings.location: What section — CONTEXT ASSEMBLY; det_floor.py `PlanContext` docstring (line 96–98)
findings.suggested_fix: Pass the bounded parent plan text as `extra_context` to `pass1_chunk` (the existing mechanism already used for G5's decomposition state block), bypassing the need to add a truncated field to `PlanContext` at all. This keeps `PlanContext` whole-content, reuses the exact injection seam, and is aligned with how `decomposition_state_block` already works.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a positive rationale statement explaining why parent plan text is pre-assembled into `PlanContext` at orchestrator level rather than fetched by the G7 agent directly — specifically state whether agent-tier runners have ticket-store read tools in their agentic loop.
findings.finding: The plan provides no rationale for WHY the parent plan text must be pre-assembled into `PlanContext` (with orchestrator-level store reads) rather than having the G7 AGENT fetch the parent ticket directly via its existing tool-using capability — the same way E4/G1G2/A1/G6 read the codebase via their agentic tools. If the AGENT tier already has ticket-store read tools, the `parent_plan` field on `PlanContext` is redundant complexity; if agents cannot read the ticket store (their tools are code-scoped, not store-scoped), the injection is necessary — but this distinction is not stated.
findings.location: What section — CONTEXT ASSEMBLY (NEW mechanism) and MECHANISM CORRECTNESS; also the positive-rationale requirement

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State an explicit default char cap (e.g., `2000` characters per ancestor level) for the `parent_plan` truncation in the What/Scope sections so the PlanContext field contract and the context-assembly test can anchor to a concrete value.
findings.finding: The char cap for truncating `parent_plan` context (parent + grandparent title/description) is referenced as 'a char cap' without specifying the actual value. An executor must choose a number, making AC5's bounding assertion and the PlanContext field contract under-specified.
findings.location: What section — 'BOUNDED (truncate each to a char cap; no whole-subtree dumps)' and Scope — 'det_floor.py `PlanContext` (add `parent_plan` field)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the What section: 'BOUNDED: each ancestor's title+description is truncated to 2000 characters (a named constant `PARENT_PLAN_CHAR_CAP = 2000` in det_floor.py); the test asserts the injected parent_plan field does not exceed 2 × PARENT_PLAN_CHAR_CAP.' Adjust the constant if existing G-series context budgets suggest a different ceiling.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify error-handling for the parent-chain store reads in `_assemble_context_uncached`: define the fallback when a parent ticket is missing or the read fails (e.g., set `parent_plan` to `None`/empty and skip G7 with an advisory note, never propagate the exception to other criteria).
findings.finding: The plan introduces a new store read inside `orchestrator._assemble_context_uncached` (parent + grandparent ticket lookup) but specifies no error-handling or fallback for the case where the parent ticket is absent, deleted, or the store read fails. An unhandled exception here would propagate into the plan-review pipeline for every leaf ticket, potentially blocking reviews unrelated to the G7 criterion.
findings.location: What section — 'CONTEXT ASSEMBLY (NEW mechanism)'; orchestrator.py `_assemble_context_uncached`; AC: 'The parent's plan text is injected into the criterion context'
findings.suggested_fix: In `_assemble_context_uncached`, wrap the parent-chain lookups in a try/except (or None-guard after a store.get call); if the parent ticket is not found or the read fails, set `parent_plan = None` and log a warning. In the G7 routing `applies_at` gate, also skip the criterion when `parent_plan is None`, so a missing parent degrades gracefully rather than erroring or producing a spurious finding.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the fallback for a failed parent-chain store read in `_assemble_context_uncached`: e.g. 'on any exception reading the parent ticket, set `parent_plan` to empty string, log a warning, and continue (G7 then behaves as PASS not-applicable due to the absent parent context)'.
findings.finding: The plan specifies populating `parent_plan` in `_assemble_context_uncached` 'by reading the parent chain from the store' but provides no fallback for a failed parent store read. Every other store read in `_assemble_context_uncached` has an explicit fail-open path (children enumeration failure logs a warning and sets `listed = []`; per-child fetch falls back to the summary). A failed `show_ticket(parent_id)` is unaddressed, leaving the behavior undefined.
findings.location: CONTEXT ASSEMBLY section — 'populate it in orchestrator._assemble_context_uncached (orchestrator.py:163) by reading the parent chain from the store' / orchestrator.py _assemble_context_uncached lines 163–211
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the CONTEXT ASSEMBLY section: 'Wrap each show_ticket(parent_id) call in a try/except; on failure, log a warning and set parent_plan to empty string — consistent with the existing per-child fallback pattern in _assemble_context_uncached.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Name the character cap for each injected parent/grandparent plan text (e.g. `PARENT_PLAN_CHAR_CAP = 4000` per level) as a named constant in the plan so the implementer has a non-arbitrary starting point.
findings.finding: The char cap for the bounded parent+grandparent context injection is never specified. The plan says 'BOUNDED (truncate each to a char cap)' but gives no value. This is a concrete processing parameter the implementer of `PlanContext.parent_plan` and `_assemble_context_uncached` needs to make a non-arbitrary decision.
findings.location: What / CONTEXT ASSEMBLY section — 'BOUNDED (truncate each to a char cap; no whole-subtree dumps)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the CONTEXT ASSEMBLY section: 'Use a cap of 4 000 chars per level (parent + grandparent); store the cap as a module-level constant `PARENT_PLAN_CHAR_CAP` so it is tunable.' This is consistent with the P4/P8 named constants.

findings.finding: Acceptance-criteria / definition-of-done quality issues.
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Name the truncation char cap as a named constant or config key (not a magic number), specify its initial value and rationale in the plan, and note it as a tuning target in the promotion-decision comment.
findings.finding: The char cap for truncating parent and grandparent plan text is described only as 'truncate each to a char cap' with no specified value, named constant, or config key. There is no derivation or measurement plan for choosing the cap — it is an asserted default that affects LLM context quality and token cost, and is likely to need tuning post-deployment.
findings.location: What section — 'BOUNDED (truncate each to a char cap; no whole-subtree dumps)'; Scope — det_floor.py PlanContext
findings.suggested_fix: Define a module-level constant such as `PARENT_PLAN_CHAR_CAP = 2000` (or equivalent) in det_floor.py with a comment explaining it is the per-ancestor truncation limit. Reference this constant from the orchestrator assembly code and document it in the criterion guide so operators know it is tunable without a code search.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a failure-path test (and specify the intended behavior) for orchestrator._assemble_context_uncached when parent_id is present but the parent ticket is missing from the store, and when the grandparent lookup returns None — covering the 'orphaned parent' and 'partial chain' cases.
findings.finding: The context-assembly path that reads the parent chain from the store (orchestrator._assemble_context_uncached) has no specified failure-path test: the plan's test suite does not address what happens when parent_id references a ticket that no longer exists in the store, or when the grandparent lookup returns None/missing. This is new logic invoked on every leaf-ticket claim and is untested for its error/absent-parent branch.
findings.location: Scope / AC5: 'a test asserting the assembly'; What: 'populate it in orchestrator._assemble_context_uncached ... by reading the parent chain from the store'
findings.suggested_fix: In the AC for the context-assembly test, add: 'including a case where parent_id references a non-existent ticket (expect parent_plan to be None or empty string, not an exception)' and specify in the What section that the orchestrator silently degrades to parent_plan=None if any ancestor lookup fails, so G7 skips or runs without parent context rather than erroring.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm whether `parent_plan` belongs on `PlanContext` (shared struct, assembled for every ticket) vs. being fetched inline by the G7 agent at runtime (consistent with how G1G2/E4/A1 fetch their context), citing the Rule-of-Three against existing AGENT-tier context-injection patterns.
findings.finding: Adding `parent_plan` as a field on `PlanContext` has exactly one consumer (G7), violating Rule-of-Three for a shared struct field. The established AGENT-tier pattern (G1G2, E4, A1, G6) is to fetch criterion-specific context via tools at runtime — none of these criteria pre-populate PlanContext fields for their own context needs. G7 (exec:AGENT) can call `show_ticket(parent_id)` directly, just as other AGENT criteria grep/glob the codebase.
findings.location: Scope section: 'det_floor.py PlanContext (add parent_plan field)' and 'orchestrator.py _assemble_context_uncached (populate it from the parent chain)'
findings.suggested_fix: Have the G7 rubric prompt instruct the agent to call `show_ticket(parent_id)` to fetch the parent plan, exactly as other AGENT criteria call `grep`/`glob`/`resolve_symbol`. This avoids the PlanContext field, the `_assemble_context_uncached` change, and the truncation constant entirely — the agent reads what it needs, bounded naturally by its context window.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify that ADR-0036 defines the expand-contract advisory-first posture pattern (or correct the ADR citation to the actual policy document that does), and add the correct reference to the AC that requires documenting the promotion-decision path.
findings.finding: The plan cites 'ADR-0036 expand-contract house pattern' as the policy basis for shipping G7 at advisory (0.95), but a codebase search finds ADR-0036 referenced only in `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/dispatch_one.py:111` for jittered backoff — not for an expand-contract posture pattern. If ADR-0036 does not define the advisory-first posture pattern, the plan rests on an unverifiable (possibly misidentified) policy document, and the implementer has no authoritative source for the promotion-decision path it is asked to document.
findings.location: What section: 'POSTURE: ship `default_posture: advisory` at 0.95 per the ADR-0036 expand-contract house pattern'
findings.suggested_fix: Check whether the expand-contract posture pattern is documented under a different ADR number (e.g. the ADR mentioned in the existing T4 overlay or the `review_kernel/verify.py` expand-contract comment at line 200). Update the plan to cite the correct ADR, or add a brief inline rationale if no ADR codifies this pattern yet.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC (or extend AC#1/AC#6) explicitly verifying that `docs/plan-review-criteria-guide.md` is regenerated and reflects the new G7 entry (e.g., assert the guide contains 'G7' or 'parent-containment').
findings.finding: The Scope names `docs/plan-review-criteria-guide.md` regeneration as a deliverable, but no AC covers it — an implementer could skip it and all ACs would still pass.
findings.location: Scope section (last sentence before test list); Acceptance Criteria (AC1–AC8)
findings.suggested_fix: Add: '- [ ] `docs/plan-review-criteria-guide.md` is regenerated and contains an entry for G7/parent-containment (a grep or snapshot test is sufficient).' — or fold this into the existing `validate_packaged_routing()==[]` test cluster.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify that `parent_plan` in `PlanContext` is Optional with a default of `None` (or equivalent), and confirm existing `PlanContext` construction callsites require no modification — or document the migration/rollback path for any callsite that must change.
findings.finding: The plan adds a `parent_plan` field to `det_floor.PlanContext` but does not specify whether it is Optional/defaulted, risking silent breaking of existing callsites that construct `PlanContext` without the new field.
findings.location: What section / CONTEXT ASSEMBLY; Scope: 'det_floor.py PlanContext (add parent_plan field)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the CONTEXT ASSEMBLY bullet: 'parent_plan: Optional[str] = None — existing callsites that omit this field are unaffected (backward-compatible addition).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an ADR or an explicit section in `docs/plan-review-criteria-guide.md` (or CLAUDE.md) documenting the ancestor-context injection pattern — the `parent_plan` PlanContext field, its population contract, char-cap semantics, and the rule that only G7 (and future explicitly-declared criteria) consume it.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a new cross-component pipeline contract — a `parent_plan` field on `PlanContext` (det_floor.py), populated in `orchestrator._assemble_context_uncached`, and surfaced via `pass1._ticket_graph_blob` — but names no ADR or design-doc update capturing this architectural decision. This pattern (injecting ancestor plan text into review context) is novel within the plan-review system and establishes a precedent future criteria will follow; without a doc artifact, the convention is undiscoverable.
findings.location: What section — context-assembly NEW mechanism; Scope — det_floor.py, orchestrator.py, pass1.py
findings.suggested_fix: Extend the scoped `docs/plan-review-criteria-guide.md` regeneration to include a 'Criterion Context Contract' section covering the `parent_plan` field: what it contains (parent + grandparent title/description, bounded), how it is populated (orchestrator reads parent chain from store), the char cap value/config key, and which criteria consume it. Alternatively, file a micro-ADR (ADR-XXXX) capturing the ancestor-context injection decision and reference it from the guide.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm registry.py line 66 (`CODEBASE_GROUNDED` frozenset) is explicitly in the implementer's edit checklist — the Scope section mentions it in the frozenset names but the specific symbol line should be a named edit target alongside `CANONICAL_LLM` and `AGENT_TIER`.
findings.finding: The plan does not name `CODEBASE_GROUNDED` (registry.py line 66: `frozenset({"E4", "G1G2", "A1", "G6"})`) as an edit target. G7 is `exec: AGENT` and reads the parent ticket from the store — exactly the profile of the existing `CODEBASE_GROUNDED` members. Omitting G7 from `CODEBASE_GROUNDED` would leave `workflow_ops.py`'s `code_grounded` flag and the `drift_refresh` probe set inconsistent with the new criterion.
findings.location: Scope section — 'register G7 in CANONICAL_LLM/AGENT_TIER/CODEBASE_GROUNDED'; registry.py line 66
findings.suggested_fix: Add `CODEBASE_GROUNDED` frozenset (registry.py line 66) explicitly to the Scope file-edit list alongside the `CANONICAL_LLM` and `AGENT_TIER` entries.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add the parent-realign move's `name`, `template` text (encoding the parent-wins rule and the 'update the parent first' directive), and `applies_when: ["G7"]` to the plan's Scope section so the MOVE_REGISTRY entry is fully specified before implementation.
findings.finding: The new parent-realign MOVE_REGISTRY entry for Pass-4 is mentioned — 'the coach MOVE_REGISTRY (one parent-realign move in passes.py)' — but its template, name, and move id are entirely unspecified. The existing `MOVE_REGISTRY` in passes.py:729 uses a concrete `{id: {name, template, applies_when?}}` schema; the plan provides none of the three required fields for the new move, leaving the executor to invent the vocabulary of the coaching intervention.
findings.location: Scope section — 'the coach MOVE_REGISTRY (one parent-realign move in passes.py)' / passes.py MOVE_REGISTRY lines 729–792
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the plan: 'Move id `"13"`: `{"name": "parent-realign", "template": "Realign {subject} to the parent\'s declared scope — the parent wins on conflict. If the parent is wrong, update the parent first, then re-review this leaf.", "applies_when": ["G7"]}`'

findings.finding: The parent-plan context assembly requires fetching the parent ticket's description at review time, but neither `orchestrator.py`'s `_assemble_context_uncached` nor `det_floor.py`'s `PlanContext` are named as edit targets. The AC states 'The parent's plan text is injected into the criterion context (bounded to parent + grandparent), with a test asserting the assembly,' implying a real assembly-side code change, yet the Scope section lists only `criteria_routing.json`, `registry.py`, the rubric prompt, and the move registry.
findings.location: Scope section / AC5 / src/rebar/llm/plan_review/orchestrator.py (_assemble_context_uncached) and src/rebar/llm/plan_review/det_floor.py (PlanContext)

findings.finding: The plan's context-assembly spec says parent plan text is injected 'the same way the ancestor carve-out reads ancestors — bounded, no whole-subtree dumps' and references commit '8a9bb4308', but no such ancestor carve-out mechanism exists in the codebase. `_ticket_graph_blob` injects only the parent's ticket ID (a string), not its plan text. No existing code path fetches and injects a parent's description/title/ACs into an individual criterion's agentic context window.
findings.location: What section — CONTEXT ASSEMBLY paragraph / AC line 5 ('parent's plan text is injected into the criterion context')

findings.finding: The plan asserts context assembly should inject parent plan text 'the same way the ancestor carve-out reads ancestors,' implying a pre-existing named context-injection mechanism for ancestors. No such mechanism exists in pass1.py or the orchestrator — the G1G2 'ancestor carve-out' is purely an agentic LLM instruction (rubric text telling the agent to treat depends_on targets as existing), not a pre-assembled context-injection pipeline. The plan's 'same way' invocation describes a non-existent implementation pattern.
findings.location: CONTEXT ASSEMBLY section: 'inject the parent's title/description (and the parent's own parent one level up, if present) into the criterion's context the same way the ancestor carve-out reads ancestors — bounded, no whole-subtree dumps'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
